channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula diz que Tarcísio recebe mal prefeitos de São Paulo
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that São Paulo mayors are poorly received by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, suggesting they should first seek solutions at the state level before going to Brasília. He emphasized that the federal government receives all mayors with dignity, regardless of ideology, during the 17th Federative Caravan at Expo Center Norte.

item.title: Tarcísio se reúne com Moraes, Gilmar e Fachin em meio a ofensiva por domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas is meeting with STF ministers this Thursday to discuss judicial actions of interest to the state, including the possibility of house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro. The meeting follows Bolsonaro’s hospitalization, which revived efforts by his defense to persuade Minister Alexandre de Moraes to reconsider the house arrest request, previously denied on the grounds that the Papuda prison can meet his medical needs. Although the official agenda includes the privatization of Sabesp, Bolsonaro’s health condition is expected to be a central topic of discussion.

item.title: Brasil envia ajuda a Cuba em meio a cerco dos EUA
item.description: Brazil has sent 20,000 tons of rice, along with other food and medicine, to assist Cuba amid a worsening crisis following the cutoff of Venezuelan oil supplies after the U.S. detained Nicolás Maduro. The Brazilian government denounced the situation as an 'ideological persecution' and stated the aid is being delivered through the World Food Programme. The shipment includes husked and milled rice, black beans, powdered milk, and medicines, with initial medical supplies arriving by air.

item.title: Lula confirma presença em jantar de arrecadação do PT
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva confirmed his attendance at a fundraising dinner for the Workers' Party (PT), reinforcing his support for the party amid preparations for the 2026 elections. The event aims to raise funds for state and federal campaigns, with emphasis on mobilizing the party’s base. Lula’s confirmation is seen as a signal of strengthening the PT’s political articulation for the upcoming election.

item.title: Me preocupo com antecipar coisa sem necessidade, diz Flávio sobre divulgação de plano de governo
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, said he fears prematurely unveiling projects without necessity when releasing his government plan. He emphasized that he prefers to avoid premature exposure of proposals to prevent unrealistic expectations or unnecessary wear and tear. The statement was made on Thursday, March 19, 2026.

item.title: Em evento em São Paulo, Lula confirma Dario Durigan como substituto de Haddad
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva confirmed during an event in São Paulo that Dario Durigan, the executive secretary of the Ministry of Finance, will replace Fernando Haddad as head of the ministry, as Haddad prepares to run for governor of São Paulo. Lula urged the public to get to know Durigan, stating he would be held accountable for economic outcomes. Haddad, also present, reflected on his tenure, thanking Congress and emphasizing the restored federal pact as key to Brazil’s economic progress.

item.title: Ocaso da democracia dos EUA sob Trump é má notícia para o Brasil
item.description: The annual V-Dem report, a leading global monitor of democracy, confirms the accelerated deterioration of democracy in the United States under Donald Trump’s government, labeling it a significant democratic backslide. This trend is viewed as bad news for Brazil, as it weakens a key pillar of the liberal international order and may embolden similar authoritarian movements in other countries, including Brazil.

item.title: Estados devem recusar proposta do governo para reduzir ICMS nos combustíveis; Fazenda diz que está disposta a negociar
item.description: State governors are rejecting the federal government's proposal to zero the ICMS tax on diesel imports until May, citing concerns over state revenue and fiscal autonomy. Rio Grande do Sul has presented a counterproposal for discussion at the Confaz, while the Finance Ministry says it remains open to negotiation. President Lula reiterated the call for ICMS reduction on fuels, linking it to the effects of the Middle East war.

item.title: Lula abre palanque para Haddad e diz que prefeitos de SP são mal recebidos por Tarcísio
item.description: Lula cited criticisms from mayors against Governor Tarcísio de Freitas on the same day he is expected to officially announce Fernando Haddad as the pre-candidate for the São Paulo government.

item.title: A surpresa de Valdemar ao tratar dos planos eleitorais de Hélio Lopes
item.description: PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto was surprised to learn that Deputy Hélio Lopes plans to change his electoral domicile to Roraima to run for Senate, following Jair Bolsonaro’s guidance. This move could disrupt the PL’s electoral alliances in the state ahead of the October elections. Valdemar attempted to dissuade him, noting that Lopes’ TCU candidacy might help PT candidate Odair Cunha win.

item.title: Lula diz estar 'triste' com decisão do BC de reduzir taxa de juros em 'só' 0,25 ponto percentual
item.description: President Lula expressed disappointment with the Copom's decision to cut the Selic rate by only 0.25 percentage point, from 15% to 14.75%, saying he had expected a 0.5-point reduction. He blamed the central bank's caution on the effects of the Iran war, criticizing external influences on domestic monetary policy.

item.title: Moro firma compromisso com PL e se filiará ao partido para disputar governo do Paraná
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro will leave União Brasil and join the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, following a history of conflict with the party, including attempts to cassate his mandate in 2022 and 2023. His move marks a break with Governor Ratinho Junior (PSD), who is expected to run for president, and comes after meetings in Brasília with Valdemar Costa Neto and Flávio Bolsonaro, who praised Moro as the PL’s pre-candidate for Paraná governor.

item.title: Lula anuncia substituto de Haddad no Ministério da Fazenda
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad announced he will leave the Ministry of Finance this Thursday, with his removal to be published in the Official Gazette by Friday. His successor will be Dario Durigan, the current second-in-command, chosen by Lula as the natural replacement. Haddad is expected to launch his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo within the next 24 hours to challenge incumbent Tarcísio de Freitas.

item.title: Silvio Costa Filho desiste do Senado e vai concorrer à Câmara
item.description: Minister of Ports and Airports Silvio Costa Filho (Republicans) withdrew from the Senate race and announced he will run for re-election as a federal deputy in Pernambuco in 2026. The decision followed talks with President Lula, his party, and political allies, and was driven by poor polling numbers showing him at 10%-13% voter intent, behind Marília Arraes and Humberto Costa.

item.title: Haddad confirma saída da Fazenda e faz balanço da gestão exaltando pacto federativo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad confirmed his departure from the ministry and presented a review of his economic management, emphasizing Congress’s role and the federal pact in driving recovery. He cited policies such as raising the income tax exemption threshold to R$ 5,000 and taxing high incomes, including previously exempt dividends, while praising cross-partisan support for states through initiatives like Propag. Haddad is also expected to run for governor of São Paulo in 2026 at Lula’s request.

item.title: Cláudio Castro decidiu renunciar ao governo do Rio para evitar cassaçăo, dizem aliados | Casado
item.description: Three allies of Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) stated he decided to resign from office to avoid a potential removal by the TSE, whose trial is set to resume on Tuesday (24). The information was reported by UOL columnist Leticia Casado, based on sources close to the governor. The resignation would be a strategy to prevent losing his mandate due to electoral irregularities.

item.title: Lula reforça pedido a governadores por redução no ICMS sobre combustíveis
item.description: President Lula renewed his call for governors to reduce or exempt the ICMS tax on fuels to counter domestic impacts from the Middle East war. The federal government offers to compensate half of the states' revenue loss, with a decision expected by March 28 at an in-person meeting in São Paulo.

item.title: ‘Hoje é um dia especial, estou saindo do Ministério da Fazenda’, diz Haddad
item.description: Fernando Haddad announced today that he is leaving the Ministry of Finance to run for governor of São Paulo against Tarcísio de Freitas in 2026. He highlighted his proposals to exempt individuals earning up to R$5,000 from income tax and compensate the revenue loss by taxing the super-rich, while defending the Tax Reform as a measure against injustices faced by workers.

item.title: João Campos e Raquel Lyra dão largada para eleição em PE com times definidos e disputa por apoio de Lula
item.description: Recife Mayor João Campos (PSB) has defined his majoritarian ticket for the Pernambuco gubernatorial race in October, including Senator Humberto Costa (PT) seeking re-election and former deputy Marília Arraes (PDT) running for Senate. The official announcement is expected by week’s end. The alliance signals coordination with potential support from Lula.

item.title: Lula sanciona duas leis com R$ 3,6 bi para agricultura e indústria
item.description: President Lula signed two laws allocating R$ 3.6 billion to agriculture and industry: R$ 500 million for Pronaf via the Guarantee Fund and R$ 3.1 billion in tax incentives for Cubatão’s chemical industry, reducing PIS/Pasep and Cofins through 2027. The measures were announced at the 17th Federative Caravan in São Paulo, which also included investments in health, mobility, housing, education, and public safety.

item.title: Ex-assessora de Flávio Bolsonaro é denunciada sob acusação de lavar dinheiro de filho miliciano
item.description: A former aide to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro in Alerj has been denounced for allegedly laundering money linked to Adriano da Nóbrega, the senator’s son and militia leader, who was killed in a 2022 police operation in Bahia. The accusation centers on the use of illicit militia funds in concealed financial operations.

item.title: Sob cerco de Trump, Brasil envia 21.000 toneladas de ajuda a Cuba
item.description: The Brazilian government is preparing to send 21,000 tons of humanitarian aid to Cuba, including food and medicine, following an initial discreet shipment of 2.5 tons two weeks ago. The aid responds to Cuba’s worsening humanitarian crisis marked by blackouts, fuel shortages, and healthcare collapse, worsened after Venezuela’s aligned government reduced oil shipments and Trump threatened tariffs on countries supplying fuel to Cuba. While Brazil claims the aid is apolitical, the timing during an election year and amid U.S. pressure invites geopolitical readings, especially after Trump threatened to intervene in Cuba and Díaz-Canel confirmed talks with the U.S.

item.title: Omar Aziz lidera corrida pelo governo do Amazonas, diz Real Time
item.description: Former governor Omar Aziz leads the race for Amazonas governor in all stimulated first-turn scenarios with 39% and 37%, ahead of Maria do Carmo Seffair (PL) and David Almeida (Avante). Aziz also wins in second-turn simulations against both opponents. David Almeida has the highest rejection rate at 47%, while Governor Wilson Lima's administration is disapproved by 50% of respondents.

item.title: Alesp: Partidos pedem cassação de deputada do PL por 'blackface' na tribuna
item.description: State deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL) faces cassation requests in the Ethics Council of Alesp after performing 'blackface' on the legislative floor and attacking federal deputy Érika Hilton (PSOL-SP). The racist act occurred during a session of the São Paulo Legislative Assembly. The incident provoked strong backlash from fellow lawmakers and civil society groups.

item.title: A caminho do PL, Moro foi chamado de ‘traíra sem caráter’ pelos Bolsonaros; lembre troca de ofensas
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União-PR) will join the PL on June 24 to support his gubernatorial campaign in Paraná, in exchange for providing electoral backing to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president. Despite the impending alliance, Moro and the Bolsonaro family have exchanged public insults for years, including labels like 'traitor', 'without character', ' blatant liar', and 'great traitor', with Moro accusing Bolsonaro of interfering in the Federal Police and maintaining a negationist government that dismantled anti-corruption efforts.

item.title: Capitão Alberto Neto lidera disputa ao Senado no Amazonas, diz Real Time
item.description: Captain Alberto Neto (PL) leads the Senate race in Amazonas with 23% to 24% voting intention for the first seat, according to Real Time Big Data poll. The race for the second seat is more fragmented, with Eduardo Braga (MDB) and Wilson Lima tied at 16% in the scenario including the governor. The survey interviewed 1,500 people between March 17 and 18, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Adidas e Nike lideram em fornecimento na Sul-Americana 2026
item.description: Adidas and Nike lead in sportswear supply for the 2026 Copa Sudamericana, each equipping four clubs. The tournament will feature seven Brazilian representatives, with Atlético Mineiro, São Paulo, Grêmio, and Santos in Pot 1 of the draw, scheduled for March 19, 2026. The group stage runs from April 7 to May 28, with the final set for November 21.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula abre a Caravana Federativa de São Paulo
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attends the opening of the 17th Federative Caravan in São Paulo, an event organized by the SRI featuring the delivery of vehicles and equipment for public health and security, as well as housing units. Vice President Geraldo Alckmin and Minister Gleisi Hoffmann are also present. The event, running until March 20 at Expo Center Norte, includes workshops on programs like Minha Casa Minha Vida and Comunica BR, with booths from over 30 federal ministries and agencies to assist municipal officials.

item.title: 54% desaprovam e 42% aprovam governo Lula no Amazonas, diz pesquisa
item.description: In Amazonas, 54% of voters disapprove of President Lula's government while 42% approve it, according to a Real Time Big Data poll released on March 19, 2026. The survey shows 45% rate the administration as bad or terrible, compared to 29% who see it as good or excellent. The poll interviewed 1,500 people between March 17 and 18, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: As intenções de voto para presidente no Amazonas, segundo o Real Time Big Data
item.description: In Amazonas, Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) and Lula (PT) are technically tied in the presidential race, with the former president's son leading by margins within the margin of error across three tested scenarios. Both also show the highest rejection rates, with Lula rejected by 52% and Flávio by 47% of Amazonian voters.

item.title: Deputados condenados văo se dividir entre a Câmara e a cadeia
item.description: Two federal deputies and an alternate, convicted of corruption in the use of parliamentary amendments, may continue serving their mandates under semi-open prison regimes that allow daytime work and nighttime incarceration. STF Minister Flávio Dino signaled that further severe punishments may come, as the Chamber debates whether criminal convictions are compatible with parliamentary decorum, recalling the 2017 case of Celso Jacob. The approaching 2026 elections intensify pressure to define whether convicted lawmakers can retain office while serving sentences.

item.title: Real Time Big Data: aliado de Lula lidera contra bolsonarista no maior estado do Brasil
item.description: In Amazonas, Senator Omar Aziz (PSD), an ally of Lula, leads the gubernatorial race with 37-39% voter intention, followed by bolsonarist businesswoman Maria do Carmo Seffair (PL) with 26-27%. In runoff scenarios, Aziz defeats Seffair 44%-37% and holds leads against other opponents, while the Senate race is led by federal deputy Capitão Alberto Neto (PL) at 23-24%.

item.title: PL anuncia filha de Clezão como pré-candidata a deputada federal por Goiás
item.description: The leader of the Liberal Party in the Chamber of Deputies, Sóstenes Cavalcante, announced that Luiza Cunha, daughter of businessman Cleriston Pereira da Cunha (Clezão), will be the party's pre-candidate for federal deputy from Goiás in the 2026 elections. The announcement was made alongside Senator Wilder Morais, pre-candidate for governor of Goiás, and highlighted the legacy of Clezão, who died in prison following the January 8 attacks, as a symbol of resistance and political motivation. Luiza stated she will turn her personal pain into a national cause, advocating for a Brazil that respects its people.

item.title: Luxemburgo troca o futebol pela política e oficializa pré-candidatura ao Senado
item.description: Vanderlei Luxemburgo, renowned Brazilian football coach, has officially announced his pre-candidacy for the Federal Senate from Tocantins in 2026 under the Podemos party, after turning down football offers to focus on politics. This marks his second attempt at the same seat, having been passed over by the PSB in 2022 in favor of Carlos Amastha, which he described as a 'stab in the back'. Luxemburgo stated he does not identify with current political polarization and aims to end his sports career if elected, serving an eight-year term in Brasília.

item.title: O fim de ação no STF contra sanções dos EUA a Moraes
item.description: The Attorney General's Office requested the dismissal of a case before the Supreme Federal Court seeking to block Brazilian financial institutions from enforcing U.S. sanctions against Minister Alexandre de Moraes, arguing the case lost its subject after Moraes and his wife were removed from the Magnitsky List by the Trump administration in December 2025. The action, initiated by Congressman Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ), aimed to safeguard Brazilian sovereignty from unacceptable international interference but is likely to be granted by relator Minister Cristiano Zanin. The incident occurred amid bilateral tensions that eased following direct talks between Trump and Lula.

item.title: Governo Lula torce por decisão técnica do Confaz sobre proposta para zerar ICMS sobre diesel importado
item.description: The Lula government is urging the Confaz to approve a proposal from the Ministry of Finance to zero the ICMS tax on imported diesel for April and May, at an estimated monthly cost of R$ 3 billion, half of which would be covered by the Union. The measure aims to ease the burden of rising oil prices—driven by the U.S.-Iran conflict—on truckers and consumers, despite initial opposition from state governors aligned with the opposition, who suspect the move could benefit Lula electorally.

item.title: PGR pede fim de ação no STF após EUA suspenderem sanções a Moraes
item.description: The Attorney General's Office requested the dismissal of a case at the Supreme Federal Court after the U.S. lifted sanctions against Minister Alexandre de Moraes in December 2025, arguing the case no longer has a purpose. The lawsuit, filed by federal deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ), sought to prevent Brazilian banks from applying foreign restrictions on national authorities. The case is under the rapporteurship of Minister Cristiano Zanin, who will decide whether to accept the PGR's recommendation.

item.title: Primeira sessão da Comissão da Mulher com Erika Hilton não aprova nada e tem microfone cortado
item.description: The first session of the Chamber's Women's Commission, presided over by Erika Hilton (PSOL-SP), ended without approving any proposals amid a tense atmosphere, with the Bolsonarist opposition challenging her leadership and accusing her of persecuting women. Hilton denied the allegations, stating her social media remarks targeted 'transphobes and imbeCIS', and committed to including the opposition's requests on the agenda for the next session in April.

item.title: João Campos fecha chapa com Marília Arraes e Humberto Costa e frustra planos de Raquel Lyra
item.description: Recife Mayor João Campos (PSB) secured a ticket with Marília Arraes (PDT) and Humberto Costa (PT) for the Pernambuco gubernatorial race, thwarting Raquel Lyra’s (PSD) efforts to attract the former deputy. The deal, brokered in Brasília with Lula’s backing and national PT and PDT leaders, aligns Campos with the federal government and includes Carlos Costa (Republicanos) as vice-presidential candidate. The move strengthens the PSB’s base and undermines Lyra’s Senate strategy.

item.title: O impasse entre Flávio Bolsonaro e Lula no segundo maior colégio eleitoral do Norte
item.description: In Amazonas, the second-largest electoral college in the North with nearly 2.8 million voters, the Real Time Big Data poll shows a technical tie between Flávio Bolsonaro (41-42%) and Lula (40-41%) across three first-round scenarios, within the 2-point margin of error. Ratinho Júnior leads PSD contenders with 5%, followed by Caiado (4%) and Leite (2%).

item.title: Real Time Big Data aponta favoritismo de Aziz na disputa pelo governo do Amazonas
item.description: Senator Omar Aziz (PSD) leads all simulations for the Amazonas governorship in Real Time Big Data polling, maintaining a consistent advantage over Maria do Carmo Seffair (PL) and other candidates. David Almeida (Avante) shows the highest rejection rate at 47%, while Aziz stands at 40%, signaling electoral polarization. In Senate scenarios, federal deputy Capitão Alberto Neto (PL) is the best-positioned candidate.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro e o PL ampliam a pressão sobre um Romeu Zema já cercado por todos os lados
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro and the PL are pressuring Romeu Zema to withdraw from the presidential race, offering him the vice-presidential spot on Jair Bolsonaro's ticket, as Zema faces low polling and is being surrounded by other conservative candidates. His campaign now relies more on strengthening the Novo Party in Congress than on realistic presidential hopes, especially after losing the core Bolsonarist voter base to Flávio Bolsonaro and allies like Tarcísio de Freitas and Ratinho Júnior.

item.title: Tarcísio vai a Brasília e intensifica articulação no STF por domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas traveled to Brasília to intensify lobbying at the Supreme Federal Court (STF) for house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro on humanitarian grounds, despite his official agenda being tied to the Sabesp privatization case. Bolsonaro remains in the ICU with bilateral bacterial pneumonia, showing positive clinical evolution but still requiring intensive care. The move is part of a broader effort by Bolsonaro’s allies to influence the Court, including meetings with ministers such as Gilmar Mendes, Cristiano Zanin, Flávio Dino, Luiz Fux, Edson Fachin, and potentially Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Avanços no caso Master acirram cabo de guerra por prorrogação de CPIs
item.description: The extension of the Organized Crime CPI and the INSS CPMI hinges on a decision by Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça, rapporteur of the Master case, as Congress President Davi Alcolumbre shows no willingness to convene a joint session or read the request. Opposition lawmakers see Mendonça as their last hope to prevent the commissions from ending on May 28, amid growing evidence of ties between lawyers connected to figures like Alexandre de Moraes and Antônio Rueda (União Brasil) and the Master case, involving fraud allegations and conflicts of interest.

item.title: Greve dos caminhoneiros: o que se sabe até agora sobre a ameaça de paralisação
item.description: Truckers are threatening a nationwide strike over rising diesel prices and non-compliance with the minimum freight table, awaiting official government measures before deciding in a national assembly this Thursday. The Lula administration has proposed strengthening ANTT enforcement, banning contracts with non-compliant companies, reducing ICMS on diesel, and compensating states for lost revenue, but union leaders say the proposals remain inadequate until formally published.

item.title: O aceno ao agro que Tereza Cristina orientou Flávio Bolsonaro a fazer em seu programa eleitoral
item.description: Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS), tipped as a potential running mate for Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), advised the candidate to include clear signals to agribusiness in his radio and TV electoral program. She recommended that his campaign team meet with the Confederation of Agriculture and Livestock of Brazil (CNA) to gather key data and indicators from the sector to strengthen his campaign and prepare for debates against Lula. As an agronomist and former Minister of Agriculture, she emphasized the importance of securing agribusiness support for electoral success.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro pressiona Lula por sanção da Lei Antifacção
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president in 2026, pressured President Lula to sanction or veto the Anti-Facão Law, approved by Congress and awaiting decision, with the deadline set for next Tuesday, March 24. He stated that the 'next former president of the Republic' has until that date to take a position on the matter.

item.title: Santos anuncia Cuca como novo treinador após saída de Vojvoda
item.description: Santos announced the hiring of coach Cuca to replace Juan Pablo Vojvoda, who was sacked after a 2-1 loss to Internacional. Cuca signed a contract valid until the end of 2026 and returns for his fourth stint at the club, accompanied by assistant Cuquinha and fitness coach Omar Feitosa.

item.title: “Moro está alinhado com a gente”, diz Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro declared his support for Sergio Moro’s pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná, stating that the former judge 'is aligned with us' and calling him a 'friend'. The senator and presidential pre-candidate from PL-RJ made the remark at an event on Wednesday, the 18th, emphasizing their political alignment. The statement reinforces the growing rapprochement between Bolsonarist factions and Moro, the former Justice Minister.

item.title: Lula se exime e culpa empresas por prejuízos da guerra para os eleitores
item.description: Lula blames the war between the U.S., Israel, and Iran for rising fuel prices and economic strain in Brazil, accusing the five permanent UN Security Council members of irresponsibility and business owners of profiteering from the crisis. He absolves his government of responsibility, stating that Brazilians, despite being 14,000 km from the conflict, are bearing the cost through higher diesel, gasoline, and ethanol prices, threatening his re-election prospects due to voter financial strain.
